channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: FBI Interview Reveals Alleged Comments by Prison Guard on Epstein's Death
item.description: An inmate at New York City's Metropolitan Correctional Center has told the FBI that on the morning of Jeffrey Epstein's death, he overheard a prison guard comment, 'You killed that dude,' in relation ...

item.title: Phil Gould comments on Daly Cherry-Evans’ challenges after Roosters debut
item.description: During the recent NRL match, veteran commentator and former coach Phil Gould highlighted that Daly Cherry-Evans is facing a reality check after moving to the Sydney Roosters. According to Gould, the g...

item.title: PNP boosts police presence in markets and groceries to curb hoarding and profiteering amid Middle East tensions
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) has ramped up its deployment of personnel in local markets, groceries, supermarkets, and commercial areas nationwide. This move aims to stop any possible hoarding ...

item.title: Glasgow Central Station Closed Following Fire: Travel Disruptions and Ticket Advice
item.description: Travellers in Scotland are facing significant rail disruption after a fire broke out in a vape shop adjacent to Glasgow Central station on Sunday. The station, Scotland’s busiest, remains closed until...

item.title: Managing Social and Environmental Impacts of Mineral Sand Mining in Senegal
item.description: Senegal has been producing various minerals like zircon, ilmenite, and rutile for many years. In 2007, the government granted EGC (formerly GCO) a 25-year license to mine zircon across a large coastal...

item.title: Canadian woman loses $37,000 in tuition scam involving stolen credit cards
item.description: A Vancouver woman, Mira Burgess, fell victim to a sophisticated tuition scam, losing over $37,000 in fraudulent charges to her credit and debit cards. Scammers promise discounted tuition to internatio...

item.title: Alonso cautious about Aston Martin's prospects in upcoming Chinese Grand Prix
item.description: Fernando Alonso has expressed concern over Aston Martin's ability to complete the Chinese Grand Prix, following a difficult start to the Formula One season in Australia. Both Alonso and teammate Lance...

item.title: Iran Appoints Mojtaba Khamenei as Supreme Leader Amid Escalating Regional Conflict and Economic Impact
item.description: Iran has officially appointed Mojtaba Khamenei, the son of the late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, as the country’s new supreme leader. His appointment comes during a period of escalating tensions with ...

item.title: Natalie Wilkie Wins Canada’s First Gold at 2026 Winter Paralympics
item.description: Natalie Wilkie of Salmon Arm, British Columbia, claimed Canada’s first gold medal at the 2026 Winter Paralympics in Milan-Cortina, finishing first in the women’s standing para-biathlon with a time of ...

item.title: Harry Styles makes surprise cameo in audience during Ryan Gosling's SNL monologue
item.description: During Ryan Gosling's hosting stint on Saturday Night Live on 7 March, the actor was interrupted in a humorous way by Harry Styles, who appeared in the front row of the audience. Gosling, 45, kicked o...

item.title: Tanzanian Deputy Minister encourages students in India to contribute to national development
item.description: In New Delhi, Tanzania's Deputy Minister for Foreign Affairs and East African Cooperation, Dr Ngwaru Maghembe, held a meeting with Tanzanian students studying at different universities across India. T...

item.title: DOE: Current law prevents capping of fuel prices amid global supply concerns
item.description: The Department of Energy (DOE) confirmed on March 9, 2026, that it cannot impose a ceiling on fuel prices due to the existing Oil Deregulation Law. Secretary Sharon Garin explained that unless the law...

item.title: Super Eagles Players Excel Across European Leagues
item.description: Yesterday, Super Eagles players dey make their marks for different European leagues as season dey reach critical stage. Nigeria forward Akor Adams score one goal for Sevilla FC wey help dem draw 1-1 w...

item.title: Sindh Government Advances March Salaries and Pensions for Eid Preparations
item.description: The Sindh government has decided to release the salaries, allowances, and pensions of Muslim employees and pensioners earlier than usual for the month of March. This advance payment is scheduled for M...

item.title: President Ramaphosa to Enhance South Africa-Brazil Cooperation on State Visit
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa is set to undertake a State Visit to Brazil from 9 to 10 March 2026, at the invitation of Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva. The visit aims to strengthen the hist...

item.title: Historical Perspective on Lok Sabha Speaker Impeachment and Om Birla
item.description: The news article discusses the no-confidence motion concerning Om Birla, the Speaker of the Lok Sabha, and explores whether a Speaker of India's lower house has ever been impeached in the past. While ...

item.title: ICPC Links Luxury Properties in Egypt Worth Millions of Dollars to Ex-Kaduna Governor Nasir El-Rufai Amid Corruption Probe
item.description: The Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC) don trace several luxury real estate properties for Egypt come link dem to former Kaduna State Governor, Nasir El-Rufai, ...

item.title: Sri Lanka grants one-month humanitarian visas to rescued Iranian sailors
item.description: Sri Lanka has announced that it will provide one-month visas free of cost to Iranian sailors rescued after attacks on their ships. The decision was confirmed by Minister of Public Security Ananda Wije...

item.title: Governor Douye Diri Honoured for Advancing Sports Infrastructure in Bayelsa
item.description: Bayelsa State Governor, Douye Diri, don dey gather plenty praise for him efforts wey dey transform sports infrastructure for him state. Last Friday, during the second edition of the Newstap/SWAN Five ...

item.title: Matildas held to 3-3 draw by South Korea, finish second in group at Women’s Asian Cup
item.description: Australia’s Women’s Asian Cup campaign received a wake-up call as the Matildas managed only a 3-3 draw against South Korea in front of 60,279 fans at Stadium Australia. The draw meant South Korea topp...
